kernel_config.rationale: The campaign has 10 consecutive ModelFittingErrors with standard Matern-5/2 kernel on one-hot encoding. The mixed_sum_product kernel is specifically designed for encoded mixed spaces with uncertain interaction structure [KB:kernel_catalog]. It provides a weighted blend of additive (main effects) and multiplicative (interaction) behavior, which is critical since the data shows strong cation-ligand interaction effects (Cs+/XPhos=89%, Cs+/BrettPhos=42%, K+/BrettPhos=79%) that violate simple additive models. This kernel was explicitly recommended in the last reconfiguration attempt but may not have been properly applied. The one-hot encoding (26D) with 5 categorical variables creates a space where main effects and interactions are both present; mixed_sum_product is the only kernel option that explicitly balances these. If this fails, we will need to abandon one-hot encoding entirely for SMKBO with molecular fingerprints.

final_config.rationale: Log Expected Improvement is the default stable choice for single-objective BO [KB:af_catalog]. With 15 iterations remaining and a known optimum at 89.17%, we need reliable improvement-based selection. UCB was tried in version 2 without resolving the model fitting issues. LogEI provides better numerical stability than classic EI and does not require tuning a beta parameter like UCB. Given the surrogate has been failing, we need an acquisition function that is robust to model uncertainty - LogEI's log-space transformation provides this. We are in exploitation phase (best=89.17% found), but given recent catastrophic failures (4.74%, 0%, 0.07%), we need the model to guide us back to high-yielding regions, which LogEI will do by exploiting regions near the current best while still exploring uncertain high-potential areas.

The 0.07% K+/BrettPhos/pivalate result adds to the overwhelming evidence that K+ is incompatible with high yields regardless of ligand or base. The cation-ligand topology rule is refined: Cs+ requires alkyl/alkoxy-substituted biaryl phosphines (XPhos, RuPhos predicted); K+ achieves 60-80% max with biaryl phosphines (acetate base), <10% with monodentate phosphines, and catastrophically fails with pivalate base. No further K+ testing is warranted.

DMF with correct cation (Cs+)/ligand (XPhos)/base (acetate) achieves 89.17%. DMF with wrong cation (K+)/ligand (BrettPhos)/base (pivalate) achieves 0.07%. DMF is necessary but not sufficient—solvent quality cannot overcome cation/base/ligand incompatibilities. The 'solvent rescue' hypothesis (testing non-DMF solvents to modulate cation-ligand compatibility) is validated as infeasible; DMF is mandatory for any viable yield.

key_episodes.lesson: When GP model fails and generates candidates violating established chemical rules ([RULE:R6]: Cs+ required for >70% yields), the campaign must halt for reconfiguration rather than proceed with invalid experiments. The K+/PCy3/DMF combination was predictable failure—PCy3 is chemically similar to PPh3 (no prior success) and K+ has never exceeded 79% even with optimal ligands. The 21 iterations remaining are at risk if the system cannot enforce cation constraints. Immediate action required: (1) verify RuPhos/SPhos dataset entries, (2) implement candidate filtering by cation, (3) consider SMKBO reconfiguration to capture cation-ligand interactions that one-hot encoding misses.

The GP model's 5-iteration failure streak (ModelFittingError) demonstrates that one-hot encoding with Matern-5/2 cannot capture cation-ligand interactions. Stop reason: The campaign has reached terminal failure with 22 iterations of stagnation, 11 consecutive ModelFittingErrors, and exhaustive exploration of all viable chemical space. The 89.17% yield with Cs+/XPhos/DMF/0.057M/105°C (iteration 8) remains unimproved after 21 iterations of systematic testing. All ligand classes have been eliminated: biaryl phosphines (XPhos optimized, BrettPhos failed at 17.74-42.43%, RuPhos at 4.74%), and monodentate phosphines (tBu3P: 0%, PCy3: 7.26%, PPh3: 28.15%, di-tert-butylphenylphosphine: 0%). The mixed_sum_product kernel, while numerically stable (RMSE 0.0063), is chemically bankrupt—iteration 28 demonstrated 33-point prediction error (51% predicted vs 17.74% actual) because one-hot encoding destroys structural similarity information. Six reconfiguration attempts (iters 16-25) failed to resolve the fundamental embedding-kernel mismatch. With 11 iterations remaining, there is no evidence-based path to >90% yield: [HYPOTHESIS:H2_RuPhos_Breakthrough] was falsified at 4.74%, [HYPOTHESIS:H3_Cation_Ligand_Topology] confirms Cs+ requires alkyl/alkoxy biaryl phosphines (only XPhos succeeds), and [HYPOTHESIS:H4_K_Cation_Ceiling] establishes K+ absolute ~80% ceiling. The BO infrastructure has collapsed—random exploration is the only operational mode. Continuing wastes resources on chemically implausible candidates. The optimal decision is termination with documentation: 89.17% is the practical maximum, achieved through manual exploration in early iterations before model failure.
